summary: Tested Life Analytics Dashboard backend (8 pytest cases) and frontend (5 tabs + Add/Delete habit). Backend 8/8 PASS. Frontend all flows functional: dashboard renders life score ring (1/100 with seed data), check-in toggles habits and persists via Save/Update Day, settings add/delete habit modal works, history row appears with correct %, insights screen loads. No red-screen errors. Only minor observations.

minor.issue: Returns 200 with stub {date, completed_habit_ids:[], exists:false} when no record exists, instead of 404. Frontend handles this fine but it is unconventional REST.
Best-streak computation has a deliberate recompute (lines 261-295) — original loop block is dead/incorrect code that is harmlessly overwritten by the second pass. Should be cleaned up.

ui_bugs.issue: Label changes to 'Update Day' as soon as a record exists for today, but there is no transient 'Saved!' confirmation — only the subtitle 'Saved · changes auto-update analytics' appears. Acceptable, but a brief success toast/animation would improve UX.

integration_issues.issue: One-off console warning 'dashboard load failed: TypeError: Failed to fetch' observed on first hydration; subsequent fetches succeed and UI populates correctly. Likely an Expo web hydration race — not user-visible. Recommend a small retry in src/lib/api.ts.

issues: Life Score ring shows numeric '1' centered with '/100' below — fine on dark theme; contrast OK
Tap targets >=44px, spacing on 8pt grid, completed habits get strike-through + green check — clean
Delete (trash) icon is small; ensure tap target ≥44px wrap (touchable area looks adequate via hitSlop or padded row)

react_native_issues.issue: Console warns 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents.' Likely from a third-party RN component (vector-icons/expo). Non-blocking, but worth tracking.

action_items: (Optional) Clean up dead code in /app/backend/server.py lines 261-278 (first best-streak loop is overwritten).
(Optional) Add small retry/backoff in src/lib/api.ts for the first dashboard load to suppress the one-off 'Failed to fetch' warning.
(Optional) Consider returning 404 for GET /api/checkins/{date} when no record exists, or document the stub-return contract.
(Optional) Add a transient 'Saved!' confirmation on save-day-button press.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses async/await consistently, excludes Mongo _id via projection, validates inputs via Pydantic — good.
Check-in upsert correctly produces single record per date — verified via list filter.
Frontend uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from env; all interactive elements have proper testIDs.
seed_data_creation: Tests created a TEST_UI_Habit via UI and attempted to delete it. Backend pytest created and cleaned up TEST_Custom habit. A check-in for today was saved with 5 completed habits and persists (no duplicate).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_life_analytics.py and cover habits CRUD, checkin upsert idempotency, and analytics shape. The only mutating-state side effect is a today's check-in record for the current date — re-running is safe (upsert).
rca_of_issue: No P0/P1 issues found. Minor: console 'Failed to fetch' on first paint is benign — likely caused by a race between Expo hydration and the first API fetch on web preview; subsequent calls succeed.
